<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
<title>Random walks and mixing</title>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.css">
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.js"></script>

<script>
(function(){
  function renderAll(){
    if(typeof renderMathInElement!=='function'){ return setTimeout(renderAll,50); }
    renderMathInElement(document.body,{
      delimiters:[

      }
    });
  }
  if(document.readyState==='loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',renderAll);
  else renderAll();
})();
</script>
<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  /* sticky sidebar TOC (wide screens only; narrow screens fall back to top-nav) */
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}
  table.plain th,table.plain td{padding:.35rem .5rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);text-align:left}
  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */
</style>

Random walks and mixing

Markov chains, stationary distributions, total-variation mixing, the spectral-gap bound on mixing time, the coupling method, and Markov-chain Monte Carlo. Every concept is paired with a small chain you can drive: step it, watch the empirical frequencies converge to $\pi$, and read off the cost of forgetting where you started.

blocks.html:
1. Markov chains and transition matrices

A (discrete-time, time-homogeneous) Markov chain on a state space $S$ is a stochastic process $(X_n)_{n\ge 0}$ with the memoryless property $$\mathbb{P}(X_{n+1}=y\mid X_0,\ldots,X_n)\;=\;\mathbb{P}(X_{n+1}=y\mid X_n)\;=\;P_{X_n,y}.$$ Conditional on the present, the past tells you nothing extra about the future. The numbers $P_{xy}$ assemble into a transition matrix $P\in[0,1]^{S\times S}$ which is row-stochastic: each row $P(x,\cdot)$ is a probability distribution over next states, so $\sum_y P_{xy}=1$.

The $n$-step law is the matrix power: $\mathbb{P}(X_n=y\mid X_0=x)=(P^n)_{xy}$. The space of row-stochastic matrices is closed under multiplication (Chapman–Kolmogorov $P^{m+n}=P^m P^n$), so iterating the chain is just iterating $P$.

Discrete vs.\ continuous time. Replace $P^n$ by a one-parameter semigroup $P_t=e^{tQ}$ with infinitesimal generator $Q$ (rates instead of probabilities) and you get a continuous-time chain. The phenomenology — stationary distributions, mixing, ergodicity — is parallel; we'll work in discrete time.

Worked example: a 4-state graph walk. On a graph $G=(V,E)$ the simple random walk steps from $x$ to a uniformly random neighbour: $P_{xy}=\mathbb{1}[xy\in E]/d_x$. The widget below lets you step a chip on a small graph and watches the running visit-frequency.

Random walk on a small graph
step the chip · running counts converge to $\pi_i\propto d_i$

See also
  • Markov chains (probability theory) — the general apparatus and reversibility.

Used in

<script>
/* §1 — random walk stepper on a 4-node graph */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#walk-svg'), out=$('#walk-out');
  const pos=[[160,160],[360,80],[560,160],[360,260]];
  const edges=[[0,1],[1,2],[2,3],[3,0],[1,3]];
  const labels=['A','B','C','D'];
  const N=4;
  const adj=Array.from({length:N},()=>[]);
  for(const [a,b] of edges){ adj[a].push(b); adj[b].push(a); }
  const deg=adj.map(l=>l.length);
  const totDeg=deg.reduce((a,b)=>a+b,0);
  const pi=deg.map(d=>d/totDeg);
  let state=0, counts=[1,0,0,0];
  function step(){
    const ns=adj[state];
    state = ns[Math.floor(Math.random()*ns.length)];
    counts[state]++;
  }
  function draw(){
    svg.innerHTML='';
    // edges
    for(const [a,b] of edges){
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:pos[a][0],y1:pos[a][1],x2:pos[b][0],y2:pos[b][1],
        stroke:'var(--line)','stroke-width':1.6}));
    }
    // bars below each node
    const total=counts.reduce((a,b)=>a+b,0);
    const emp=counts.map(c=>c/total);
    for(let i=0;i<N;i++){
      const cur=(state===i);
      svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:pos[i][0],cy:pos[i][1],r:cur?28:22,
        fill:cur?'var(--yellow)':'var(--panel2)',stroke:cur?'var(--yellow)':'var(--blue)','stroke-width':2}));
      const t=SVG('text',{x:pos[i][0],y:pos[i][1]+5,'text-anchor':'middle',
        'font-size':16,fill:cur?'#111':'#fff','font-weight':600});
      t.textContent=labels[i]; svg.appendChild(t);
      // empirical freq label
      const lf=SVG('text',{x:pos[i][0],y:pos[i][1]+50,'text-anchor':'middle',
        'font-size':11,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      lf.textContent=`emp ${(emp[i]*100).toFixed(1)}% / π ${(pi[i]*100).toFixed(1)}%`;
      svg.appendChild(lf);
    }
    // legend
    const cap=SVG('text',{x:360,y:30,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':13,fill:'var(--mute)'});
    cap.textContent='simple random walk · stationary $\\pi_i\\propto d_i$ · A,C have degree 2; B,D have degree 3';
    svg.appendChild(cap);
    out.textContent =
      `steps: ${total-1}, current state: ${labels[state]}\n`+
      `degrees: [${deg.join(', ')}], stationary π = [${pi.map(v=>v.toFixed(3)).join(', ')}]\n`+
      `empirical: [${emp.map(v=>v.toFixed(3)).join(', ')}]`;
  }
  $('#walk-step').addEventListener('click',()=>{step();draw();});
  $('#walk-step10').addEventListener('click',()=>{for(let k=0;k<10;k++)step();draw();});
  $('#walk-step100').addEventListener('click',()=>{for(let k=0;k<100;k++)step();draw();});
  $('#walk-reset').addEventListener('click',()=>{state=0;counts=[1,0,0,0];draw();});
  draw();
})();
</script>
</section>

2. Stationary distributions

A probability vector $\pi$ on $S$ is stationary for $P$ if $\pi P=\pi$, i.e.\ $\pi$ is fixed by one step of the chain. Equivalently, $\pi^\top$ is a right eigenvector of $P^\top$ with eigenvalue $1$. Every finite-state chain has at least one stationary $\pi$ (Brouwer / Perron–Frobenius applied to the row-stochastic matrix $P$).

Fundamental ergodic theorem (finite state). If a finite-state chain is irreducible (every state reaches every other) and aperiodic ($\gcd$ of return times to each state is $1$), then $\pi$ is unique and $P^n\to\Pi$ where every row of $\Pi$ equals $\pi$. The chain forgets its starting state.

For reversible chains, detailed balance gives $\pi$ on a plate: $\pi$ satisfies $\pi_i P_{ij}=\pi_j P_{ji}$ for all $i,j$. Summing over $i$ recovers stationarity, but reversibility is strictly stronger — asymmetric drift breaks it.

Reducible counterexample. Two disjoint closed classes give a one-parameter family of stationary distributions: any convex combination of the per-class stationaries. The widget animates the matrix powers $P^n$ side-by-side so you can watch the row-collapse.

Matrix power animator
$P^1, P^2, \ldots$ · rows collapse to $\pi$
n = 1

See also
  • Groups, rings, fields — semigroup composition $P^{m+n}=P^m P^n$.

Used in
Random walks and mixing · Mixing time

<script>
/* §2 — matrix-power animator on a 3-state chain */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#power-svg'), out=$('#power-out'), nIn=$('#pow-n'), nVal=$('#pow-nval');
  // fixed test chain: irreducible aperiodic 3-state
  const P=[[0.6,0.3,0.1],[0.2,0.5,0.3],[0.3,0.2,0.5]];
  function matmul(A,B){
    const n=A.length, m=B[0].length, k=B.length;
    const C=Array.from({length:n},()=>Array(m).fill(0));
    for(let i=0;i<n;i++) for(let j=0;j<m;j++){
      let s=0; for(let q=0;q<k;q++) s+=A[i][q]*B[q][j];
      C[i][j]=s;
    }
    return C;
  }
  function pow(M,k){
    let R=[[1,0,0],[0,1,0],[0,0,1]], B=M;
    while(k>0){ if(k&1) R=matmul(R,B); B=matmul(B,B); k>>=1; }
    return R;
  }
  function stationary(){
    let p=[1/3,1/3,1/3];
    for(let k=0;k<2000;k++){
      const np=[0,0,0];
      for(let j=0;j<3;j++) for(let i=0;i<3;i++) np[j]+=p[i]*P[i][j];
      p=np;
    }
    const s=p.reduce((a,b)=>a+b,0);
    return p.map(v=>v/s);
  }
  const pi=stationary();
  function draw(){
    svg.innerHTML='';
    const n=+nIn.value;
    const Pn=pow(P,n);
    // draw 3x3 grid representation of Pn
    const cell=68, x0=200, y0=70;
    for(let i=0;i<3;i++) for(let j=0;j<3;j++){
      const v=Pn[i][j];
      const c=Math.round(255*v);
      const fill=`rgba(255,216,102,${0.10+0.85*v})`;
      svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:x0+j*cell,y:y0+i*cell,width:cell-3,height:cell-3,
        fill, stroke:'var(--line)','stroke-width':1}));
      const t=SVG('text',{x:x0+j*cell+(cell-3)/2,y:y0+i*cell+(cell-3)/2+5,
        'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':13,fill:'#111','font-weight':600});
      t.textContent=v.toFixed(3);
      svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // π reference row beneath
    const yPi=y0+3*cell+30;
    const tlbl=SVG('text',{x:x0-10,y:yPi+18,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':12,fill:'var(--cyan)','font-weight':600});
    tlbl.textContent='π ='; svg.appendChild(tlbl);
    for(let j=0;j<3;j++){
      svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:x0+j*cell,y:yPi,width:cell-3,height:32,
        fill:`rgba(125,224,214,${0.18+0.7*pi[j]})`, stroke:'var(--cyan)','stroke-width':1.2}));
      const t=SVG('text',{x:x0+j*cell+(cell-3)/2,y:yPi+22,
        'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':13,fill:'#062825','font-weight':700});
      t.textContent=pi[j].toFixed(3);
      svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // header
    const cap=SVG('text',{x:360,y:40,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':14,fill:'var(--mute)'});
    cap.textContent=`P^${n}: each row → π as n grows (irreducible, aperiodic)`;
    svg.appendChild(cap);
    // distance
    let d=0;
    for(let i=0;i<3;i++) for(let j=0;j<3;j++) d=Math.max(d,Math.abs(Pn[i][j]-pi[j]));
    out.textContent =
      `n = ${n}\n`+
      `stationary π = [${pi.map(v=>v.toFixed(4)).join(', ')}]\n`+
      `max | (Pⁿ)_ij − π_j | = ${d.toFixed(5)}`;
    nVal.textContent=`n = ${n}`;
  }
  nIn.addEventListener('input',draw);
  draw();
})();
</script>
</section>

3. Mixing time

How fast does $P^n(x,\cdot)$ approach the stationary $\pi$? Quantify the gap with total variation distance: $$\|\mu-\nu\|_{\mathrm{TV}}\;=\;\tfrac{1}{2}\sum_{x\in S}|\mu(x)-\nu(x)|\;=\;\sup_{A\subseteq S}|\mu(A)-\nu(A)|.$$ The factor of $\tfrac{1}{2}$ converts the $L^1$ norm into the supremum-of-events characterisation. TV distance is a metric on probability measures, takes values in $[0,1]$, and is monotone non-increasing along the chain (the data-processing inequality).

Define the worst-case TV from start state $x$ at time $t$: $$d(t)\;=\;\max_{x\in S}\;\|P^t(x,\cdot)-\pi\|_{\mathrm{TV}}.$$ Then the mixing time is $$t_{\mathrm{mix}}(\varepsilon)\;=\;\min\{t\ge 0 : d(t)\le\varepsilon\}.$$ The convention $t_{\mathrm{mix}}=t_{\mathrm{mix}}(1/4)$ is standard (any constant works, by submultiplicativity $d(s+t)\le 2d(s)d(t)$).

Why $1/4$? Submultiplicativity of $d(t)$ implies $d(kt_{\mathrm{mix}})\le 2^{-k}$. So $t_{\mathrm{mix}}(\varepsilon)\le \lceil\log_2(1/\varepsilon)\rceil\,t_{\mathrm{mix}}$. The threshold constant only affects $\log(1/\varepsilon)$ factors, not the order of $t_{\mathrm{mix}}$.

<script>
/* §3 — d(t) plot vs t for a random walk on a small graph */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#tv-svg'), out=$('#tv-out'), epsIn=$('#tv-eps'), epsVal=$('#tv-epsval'), gSel=$('#tv-graph');
  const LIB={
    cycle:(()=>{
      const n=8, pos=[]; for(let k=0;k<n;k++){const a=-Math.PI/2+2*Math.PI*k/n; pos.push([Math.cos(a),Math.sin(a)]);}
      const edges=[]; for(let k=0;k<n;k++) edges.push([k,(k+1)%n]); return {n,pos,edges};
    })(),
    k6:(()=>{
      const n=6, pos=[]; for(let k=0;k<n;k++){const a=-Math.PI/2+2*Math.PI*k/n; pos.push([Math.cos(a),Math.sin(a)]);}
      const edges=[]; for(let i=0;i<n;i++) for(let j=i+1;j<n;j++) edges.push([i,j]); return {n,pos,edges};
    })(),
    dumbbell:(()=>{
      const n=8, pos=[[0,0],[1,0],[1,1],[0,1],[3,0],[4,0],[4,1],[3,1]];
      const edges=[[0,1],[0,2],[0,3],[1,2],[1,3],[2,3],[4,5],[4,6],[4,7],[5,6],[5,7],[6,7],[1,4]];
      return {n,pos,edges};
    })()
  };
  function buildP(G){
    const n=G.n;
    const A=Array.from({length:n},()=>Array(n).fill(0));
    for(const [a,b] of G.edges){ A[a][b]=1; A[b][a]=1; }
    const D=A.map(r=>r.reduce((s,x)=>s+x,0));
    // lazy walk: P = (I + D⁻¹ A) / 2 to ensure aperiodicity
    const P=Array.from({length:n},(_,i)=>Array.from({length:n},(_,j)=>{
      const w = D[i]>0?A[i][j]/D[i]:0;
      return (i===j? 0.5 : 0) + 0.5*w;
    }));
    const totDeg=D.reduce((s,x)=>s+x,0);
    const pi=D.map(d=>d/totDeg);
    return {P,pi};
  }
  function tvDist(p,q){
    let s=0; for(let i=0;i<p.length;i++) s+=Math.abs(p[i]-q[i]); return 0.5*s;
  }
  function compute(G,Tmax){
    const {P,pi}=buildP(G);
    const n=G.n;
    let worst=Array(Tmax+1).fill(0);
    // start from each state, track TV at each time
    for(let start=0;start<n;start++){
      let p=Array(n).fill(0); p[start]=1;
      worst[0]=Math.max(worst[0], tvDist(p,pi));
      for(let t=1;t<=Tmax;t++){
        const np=Array(n).fill(0);
        for(let j=0;j<n;j++){
          let s=0; for(let i=0;i<n;i++) s+=p[i]*P[i][j];
          np[j]=s;
        }
        p=np;
        worst[t]=Math.max(worst[t], tvDist(p,pi));
      }
    }
    return {worst,pi};
  }
  function draw(){
    svg.innerHTML='';
    const G=LIB[gSel.value];
    const Tmax=80;
    const {worst,pi}=compute(G,Tmax);
    const eps=+epsIn.value;
    epsVal.textContent=`ε = ${eps.toFixed(2)}`;
    // axes
    const W=720, H=320, L=70, R=40, T=30, B=50;
    const px=(t)=>L+(W-L-R)*(t/Tmax);
    const py=(v)=>T+(H-T-B)*(1-v);
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:L,y1:py(0),x2:W-R,y2:py(0),stroke:'var(--line)','stroke-width':1}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:L,y1:py(0),x2:L,y2:py(1),stroke:'var(--line)','stroke-width':1}));
    // y ticks
    for(const v of [0,0.25,0.5,0.75,1]){
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:L-3,y1:py(v),x2:L,y2:py(v),stroke:'var(--mute)','stroke-width':1}));
      const t=SVG('text',{x:L-8,y:py(v)+4,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':11,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent=v.toFixed(2); svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // x ticks
    for(const tx of [0,20,40,60,80]){
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:px(tx),y1:py(0),x2:px(tx),y2:py(0)+3,stroke:'var(--mute)','stroke-width':1}));
      const t=SVG('text',{x:px(tx),y:py(0)+18,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent=tx; svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // ε threshold
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:L,y1:py(eps),x2:W-R,y2:py(eps),
      stroke:'var(--pink)','stroke-width':1.3,'stroke-dasharray':'4 4'}));
    const epsLab=SVG('text',{x:W-R-4,y:py(eps)-5,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':12,fill:'var(--pink)'});
    epsLab.textContent=`ε = ${eps.toFixed(2)}`; svg.appendChild(epsLab);
    // d(t) curve
    let d='';
    for(let t=0;t<=Tmax;t++) d += (t===0?`M${px(t)} ${py(worst[t])}`:` L${px(t)} ${py(worst[t])}`);
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--yellow)','stroke-width':2}));
    // mixing time
    let tmix=null;
    for(let t=0;t<=Tmax;t++) if(worst[t]<=eps){ tmix=t; break; }
    if(tmix!==null){
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:px(tmix),y1:py(0),x2:px(tmix),y2:py(eps),
        stroke:'var(--cyan)','stroke-width':1.4,'stroke-dasharray':'3 3'}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:px(tmix),cy:py(eps),r:5,fill:'var(--cyan)'}));
      const lbl=SVG('text',{x:px(tmix)+8,y:py(eps)-8,'text-anchor':'start','font-size':12,fill:'var(--cyan)'});
      lbl.textContent=`t_mix(ε) = ${tmix}`; svg.appendChild(lbl);
    }
    // axis labels
    const yL=SVG('text',{x:18,y:T+(H-T-B)/2,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)',
      transform:`rotate(-90,18,${T+(H-T-B)/2})`});
    yL.textContent='d(t) = max_x ‖P^t(x,·) − π‖_TV'; svg.appendChild(yL);
    const xL=SVG('text',{x:(L+W-R)/2,y:H-12,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
    xL.textContent='time t (lazy walk steps)'; svg.appendChild(xL);
    out.textContent =
      `graph: ${gSel.options[gSel.selectedIndex].textContent}\n`+
      `stationary π = [${pi.map(v=>v.toFixed(3)).join(', ')}]\n`+
      `t_mix(${eps.toFixed(2)}) = ${tmix===null?'>'+Tmax:tmix}    d(80) = ${worst[Tmax].toFixed(4)}`;
  }
  epsIn.addEventListener('input',draw);
  gSel.addEventListener('change',draw);
  draw();
})();
</script>
</section>

4. Spectral gap and rapid mixing

For a reversible chain (i.e.\ $\pi_i P_{ij}=\pi_j P_{ji}$), the transition matrix $P$ is self-adjoint with respect to the weighted inner product $\langle f,g\rangle_\pi=\sum_i \pi_i f_i g_i$. So $P$ has real eigenvalues $$1=\lambda_1\;>\;\lambda_2\;\ge\;\cdots\;\ge\;\lambda_n\;\ge\;-1.$$ The spectral gap is $\gamma=1-\lambda_2$ (the relaxation gap), and the absolute spectral gap is $\gamma_*=1-\max(|\lambda_2|,|\lambda_n|)$.

Spectral mixing-time bound (reversible). $$\frac{\gamma_*}{2}\,\log\!\frac{1}{2\varepsilon}\;\le\;t_{\mathrm{mix}}(\varepsilon)\;\le\;\frac{1}{\gamma_*}\,\log\!\frac{1}{\pi_{\min}\varepsilon},$$ where $\pi_{\min}=\min_i\pi_i$. Big gap ⇒ fast mixing; tiny gap ⇒ either a near-disconnection ($\lambda_2\to 1$, Cheeger) or a near-bipartite oscillation ($\lambda_n\to -1$).

Why does $\lambda_2$ control the rate? Decompose any starting distribution in the basis of $P$-eigenvectors $u_1,\ldots,u_n$. The component along $u_1\propto\pi$ is preserved; every other component $\langle p,u_k\rangle$ decays by a factor of $\lambda_k$ per step. After $t$ steps the surviving contribution is at most $|\lambda_{\max\;{\rm nontrivial}}|^t$ in $\ell^2(\pi)$ — Cauchy–Schwarz then converts this to TV.

The two slow modes. $\lambda_2$ near $1$ marks a slow geometric mode that pools mass on one side of a sparse cut (Cheeger's inequality is the rigorous version). $\lambda_n$ near $-1$ marks a parity oscillation: the walk alternates between halves of a near-bipartite graph, and the parity needs many steps to relax.

/* §4 — eigenvalue inspector for a few small graphs */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#eig-svg'), out=$('#eig-out'), gSel=$('#eig-graph');
  const LIB={
    cycle:{n:8,edges:(()=>{const e=[]; for(let k=0;k<8;k++) e.push([k,(k+1)%8]); return e;})()},
    k6:{n:6,edges:(()=>{const e=[]; for(let i=0;i<6;i++) for(let j=i+1;j<6;j++) e.push([i,j]); return e;})()},
    path:{n:6,edges:[[0,1],[1,2],[2,3],[3,4],[4,5]]},
    dumbbell:{n:8,edges:[[0,1],[0,2],[0,3],[1,2],[1,3],[2,3],[4,5],[4,6],[4,7],[5,6],[5,7],[6,7],[1,4]]},
    bip33:{n:6,edges:[[0,3],[0,4],[0,5],[1,3],[1,4],[1,5],[2,3],[2,4],[2,5]]}
  };
  // eigenvalues of a symmetric matrix via Jacobi rotation
  function jacobi(M0){
    const n=M0.length;
    const A=M0.map(r=>r.slice());
    const V=Array.from({length:n},(_,i)=>{const r=Array(n).fill(0); r[i]=1; return r;});
    for(let sweep=0;sweep<200;sweep++){
      let off=0;
      for(let i=0;i<n;i++) for(let j=i+1;j<n;j++) off += A[i][j]*A[i][j];
      if(off<1e-14) break;
      for(let p=0;p<n-1;p++) for(let q=p+1;q<n;q++){
        const apq=A[p][q]; if(Math.abs(apq)<1e-12) continue;
        const app=A[p][p], aqq=A[q][q];
        const theta=(aqq-app)/(2*apq);
        const t = theta>=0 ? 1/(theta+Math.sqrt(1+theta*theta)) : 1/(theta-Math.sqrt(1+theta*theta));
        const c=1/Math.sqrt(1+t*t), s=t*c;
        for(let i=0;i<n;i++){
          const aip=A[i][p], aiq=A[i][q];
          A[i][p]=c*aip-s*aiq; A[i][q]=s*aip+c*aiq;
        }
        for(let j=0;j<n;j++){
          const apj=A[p][j], aqj=A[q][j];
          A[p][j]=c*apj-s*aqj; A[q][j]=s*apj+c*aqj;
        }
      }
    }
    const evals=[]; for(let i=0;i<n;i++) evals.push(A[i][i]);
    return evals.sort((a,b)=>b-a);
  }
  function buildEvs(G){
    const n=G.n;
    const A=Array.from({length:n},()=>Array(n).fill(0));
    for(const [a,b] of G.edges){A[a][b]=1; A[b][a]=1;}
    const d=A.map(r=>r.reduce((s,x)=>s+x,0));
    // P = (I + D^{-1}A)/2 (lazy)
    // similarity to symmetric: D^{1/2} P D^{-1/2} = (I + D^{-1/2} A D^{-1/2})/2
    const Sym=Array.from({length:n},(_,i)=>Array.from({length:n},(_,j)=>{
      let v = (i===j?0.5:0);
      v += 0.5 * (A[i][j] / Math.sqrt(d[i]*d[j]));
      return v;
    }));
    return jacobi(Sym);
  }
  function draw(){
    svg.innerHTML='';
    const G=LIB[gSel.value];
    const ev=buildEvs(G);
    const W=720, H=320, L=60, R=40, T=40, B=60;
    const ymin=-1, ymax=1.05;
    const py=v=>T+(H-T-B)*(1-(v-ymin)/(ymax-ymin));
    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:L,y1:py(ymin),x2:W-R,y2:py(ymin),stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:L,y1:py(ymax),x2:W-R,y2:py(ymax),stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:L,y1:py(0),x2:W-R,y2:py(0),stroke:'var(--mute)','stroke-dasharray':'3 3','stroke-width':0.8}));
    // y ticks
    for(const v of [-1,-0.5,0,0.5,1]){
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:L-3,y1:py(v),x2:L,y2:py(v),stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
      const t=SVG('text',{x:L-8,y:py(v)+4,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':11,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent=v.toFixed(2); svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // eigenvalues as ticks along x
    const xx0=L+30, xxw=W-R-L-50;
    for(let i=0;i<ev.length;i++){
      const x = xx0 + xxw*(i/(ev.length-1));
      const v = ev[i];
      const isLam1 = (i===0 && Math.abs(v-1)<1e-6);
      const isLam2 = (i===1);
      const isLamN = (i===ev.length-1);
      const colour = isLam1?'var(--cyan)':(isLam2?'var(--yellow)':(isLamN?'var(--pink)':'var(--mute)'));
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x,y1:py(0),x2:x,y2:py(v),stroke:colour,'stroke-width':2.2}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:x,cy:py(v),r:5,fill:colour}));
      const lbl=SVG('text',{x,y:py(v)-10,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11,
        fill:colour,'font-weight':isLam1||isLam2||isLamN?700:500});
      lbl.textContent=v.toFixed(3); svg.appendChild(lbl);
    }
    // band labels
    const lab1=SVG('text',{x:W-R-6,y:py(1)-4,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':11,fill:'var(--cyan)'});
    lab1.textContent='λ₁ = 1'; svg.appendChild(lab1);
    const lab2=SVG('text',{x:W-R-6,y:py(ev[1])-4,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':11,fill:'var(--yellow)'});
    lab2.textContent=`λ₂ = ${ev[1].toFixed(3)}`; svg.appendChild(lab2);
    const labN=SVG('text',{x:W-R-6,y:py(ev[ev.length-1])+16,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':11,fill:'var(--pink)'});
    labN.textContent=`λₙ = ${ev[ev.length-1].toFixed(3)}`; svg.appendChild(labN);
    // header
    const cap=SVG('text',{x:360,y:25,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':13,fill:'var(--mute)'});
    cap.textContent='spectrum of lazy walk P = (I + D⁻¹A)/2 (real, in [−1,1])';
    svg.appendChild(cap);
    const gap = 1 - Math.max(Math.abs(ev[1]), Math.abs(ev[ev.length-1]));
    const relax = 1 - ev[1];
    out.textContent =
      `graph: ${gSel.options[gSel.selectedIndex].textContent}\n`+
      `eigenvalues: [${ev.map(v=>v.toFixed(4)).join(', ')}]\n`+
      `relaxation gap γ = 1 − λ₂ = ${relax.toFixed(4)}\n`+
      `absolute gap γ* = 1 − max(|λ₂|,|λₙ|) = ${gap.toFixed(4)}\n`+
      `mixing-rate floor: t_mix ~ 1/γ* ≈ ${(1/gap).toFixed(2)} steps per ε-decade`;
  }
  gSel.addEventListener('change',draw);
  draw();
})();
</script>

5. Coupling for upper bounds on mixing

A coupling of two probability measures $\mu,\nu$ on $S$ is a joint distribution on $S\times S$ with marginals $\mu$ and $\nu$. The space of couplings is non-empty (the product $\mu\otimes\nu$ is one), and total variation distance is the optimal coupling cost: $$\|\mu-\nu\|_{\mathrm{TV}}\;=\;\inf_{(X,Y)\sim\text{coupling}}\;\mathbb{P}(X\ne Y).$$

For Markov chains, a Markovian coupling is a process $(X_t,Y_t)_{t\ge 0}$ on $S\times S$ where each marginal evolves as the chain $P$, and the chains agree forever after the first meeting time $\tau=\inf\{t:X_t=Y_t\}$. The coupling inequality $$\|\mathcal{L}(X_t)-\mathcal{L}(Y_t)\|_{\mathrm{TV}}\;\le\;\mathbb{P}(\tau>t)$$ is the workhorse of mixing-time upper bounds: design a coupling under which meeting is fast, and rapid mixing falls out.

Lazy walk on the hypercube. On $\{0,1\}^n$ the lazy walk picks a coordinate uniformly and randomises it. Couple $X,Y$ to share both choices: same coordinate, same new bit. After every coordinate has been visited at least once, $X_t=Y_t$. Coupon collector gives meeting time $\sim n\log n$, hence $t_{\mathrm{mix}}=O(n\log n)$. The actual mixing time is $\frac{1}{2}n\log n+O(n)$ (Aldous).

Card shuffling. For top-in-at-random shuffles, couple two decks by shuffling the same card to the same target. Once every card has been touched, the decks agree — coupon collector again. Riffle shuffles need a more delicate coupling and give $\sim\frac{3}{2}\log_2 n$ shuffles for $n$ cards (Bayer–Diaconis).

Coupling on the cycle
two walkers on $C_n$ · they merge when they meet

<script>
/* §5 — coupling on a cycle */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#cp-svg'), out=$('#cp-out');
  let n=10, x=0, y=5, t=0, met=null;
  function reset(){
    n = +$('#cp-n').value;
    if(n<6) n=6; if(n>20) n=20; if(n%2) n--;
    x=0; y=Math.floor(n/2); t=0; met=null;
    draw();
  }
  function step(){
    if(met!==null) return;
    // Common-step coupling: same direction for both, lazy with probability 1/2
    const move = Math.random();
    if(move < 0.5){
      // both stay
    } else {
      const dir = Math.random()<0.5 ? -1 : 1;
      x = (x+dir+n)%n;
      y = (y+dir+n)%n;
    }
    t++;
    if(x===y) met = t;

  function draw(){
    svg.innerHTML='';

      svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:pos[k][0],cy:pos[k][1],r:isMeet?16:(isX||isY?14:10),
        fill,stroke,'stroke-width':1.5}));
      const lbl=SVG('text',{x:pos[k][0],y:pos[k][1]+4,'text-anchor':'middle',
        'font-size':11,fill:isMeet||isX||isY?'#111':'var(--mute)','font-weight':600});
      lbl.textContent=k; svg.appendChild(lbl);
    }
    // legend
    const lx=80, ly=60;
    svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:lx,cy:ly,r:8,fill:'var(--yellow)',stroke:'var(--yellow)'}));
    const tX=SVG('text',{x:lx+14,y:ly+4,'font-size':12,fill:'var(--ink)'}); tX.textContent='X (start 0)';

    svg.appendChild(tY);

    svg.appendChild(tM);

        ? "not met yet · common-direction coupling preserves circular gap with prob 1/2"

6. Markov-chain Monte Carlo

Suppose we want to sample from a target distribution $\pi$ on $S$ — perhaps a posterior, a Boltzmann distribution, or a uniform distribution over a complicated combinatorial set. Direct sampling may be impossible, but MCMC sidesteps this: build a Markov chain whose stationary distribution is $\pi$, run it long enough to mix, and read off samples.

The Metropolis–Hastings recipe constructs such a chain from any irreducible proposal kernel $q(y\mid x)$. From the current state $x$, propose $y\sim q(\cdot\mid x)$, then accept with probability $$\alpha(x,y)\;=\;\min\!\Big(1,\;\frac{\pi(y)\,q(x\mid y)}{\pi(x)\,q(y\mid x)}\Big),$$ otherwise stay at $x$. Detailed balance holds by construction, so $\pi$ is stationary. For symmetric proposals ($q(y\mid x)=q(x\mid y)$, e.g.\ Metropolis, Gaussian random-walk), $\alpha=\min(1,\pi(y)/\pi(x))$.

Why mixing time is the cost. Independent samples from $\pi$ would be ideal, but consecutive MCMC samples are highly correlated. Roughly, only after $t_{\mathrm{mix}}$ steps is the chain "almost independent" of its starting point. So the cost of one effectively independent draw is $\Theta(t_{\mathrm{mix}})$, and the spectral-gap bound becomes a statistical bound on sampling cost.

The acceptance/exploration trade-off. A bold proposal (large jumps) explores the state space quickly but is often rejected when $\pi$ has narrow support; a timid proposal accepts often but barely moves. The widget below shows this on a small bimodal target.

Metropolis–Hastings on a bimodal target
slide $\sigma$ · watch acceptance and trace
σ = 1.00

See also
  • Expectation, variance, and moments — sample averages of $f(X)$ estimate $\mathbb{E}_\pi[f]$ via the ergodic theorem.

<script>
/* §6 — Metropolis-Hastings on a bimodal target */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#mh-svg'), out=$('#mh-out'), sigIn=$('#mh-sigma'), sigVal=$('#mh-sval');
  function gauss(){
    let u=0,v=0; while(u===0)u=Math.random(); while(v===0)v=Math.random();
    return Math.sqrt(-2*Math.log(u))*Math.cos(2*Math.PI*v);
  }
  function pi(x){
    // 0.5 N(-2, 0.6^2) + 0.5 N(2, 0.6^2)
    const a = Math.exp(-((x+2)*(x+2))/(2*0.36));
    const b = Math.exp(-((x-2)*(x-2))/(2*0.36));
    return 0.5*a + 0.5*b;
  }
  let chain = [0], accept = 0, total = 0;
  function step(){
    const sigma = +sigIn.value;
    const x = chain[chain.length-1];
    const y = x + sigma*gauss();
    const a = Math.min(1, pi(y)/pi(x));
    if(Math.random() < a){ chain.push(y); accept++; }
    else { chain.push(x); }
    total++;
  }
  function draw(){
    svg.innerHTML='';
    const W=720, H=320;
    // top: target density and chain histogram
    const Lx=-5, Rx=5, T1=20, B1=160, L=60, R=40;
    const px=v=>L+(W-L-R)*((v-Lx)/(Rx-Lx));
    const py1=v=>T1+(B1-T1)*(1-v);
    // axis
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:L,y1:B1,x2:W-R,y2:B1,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    // density curve (normalised so peak ≈ 1)
    let maxPi = pi(2);
    let d='';
    const NX=200;
    for(let k=0;k<=NX;k++){
      const xv = Lx + (Rx-Lx)*k/NX;
      const v = pi(xv)/maxPi;
      d += (k===0?'M':' L') + px(xv) + ' ' + py1(v);
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--cyan)','stroke-width':2}));
    // histogram
    const nbins=40;
    const bins=Array(nbins).fill(0);
    for(const v of chain){
      const idx=Math.floor((v-Lx)/(Rx-Lx)*nbins);
      if(idx>=0 && idx<nbins) bins[idx]++;
    }
    const maxB = Math.max(1,...bins);
    const bw = (W-L-R)/nbins;
    for(let k=0;k<nbins;k++){
      const h = (B1-T1) * (bins[k]/maxB) * 0.85;
      svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:L+k*bw,y:B1-h,width:bw-1,height:h,
        fill:'rgba(255,216,102,0.5)', stroke:'var(--yellow)','stroke-width':0.6}));
    }
    // header
    const cap=SVG('text',{x:(L+W-R)/2,y:14,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
    cap.textContent='target π(x) (cyan curve) vs.\\ empirical histogram (yellow)';
    svg.appendChild(cap);
    // bottom: trace
    const T2=200, B2=300;
    const py2=v=>T2+(B2-T2)*(1-(v-Lx)/(Rx-Lx));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:L,y1:B2,x2:W-R,y2:B2,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:L,y1:py2(0),x2:W-R,y2:py2(0),stroke:'var(--mute)','stroke-dasharray':'3 3'}));
    const trace = chain.slice(-400);
    let dt='';
    for(let k=0;k<trace.length;k++){
      const tx = L + (W-L-R)*(k/Math.max(1,trace.length-1));
      const ty = py2(trace[k]);
      dt += (k===0?'M':' L') + tx + ' ' + ty;
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:dt,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--violet)','stroke-width':1.2}));
    const cap2=SVG('text',{x:(L+W-R)/2,y:195,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
    cap2.textContent='chain trace (last 400 steps)';
    svg.appendChild(cap2);
    // y-axis ticks for trace
    for(const v of [-4,-2,0,2,4]){
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:L-3,y1:py2(v),x2:L,y2:py2(v),stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
      const t=SVG('text',{x:L-6,y:py2(v)+4,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':10,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent=v.toString(); svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    sigVal.textContent = `σ = ${(+sigIn.value).toFixed(2)}`;
    const accRate = total>0 ? accept/total : 0;
    out.textContent =
      `proposal width σ = ${(+sigIn.value).toFixed(2)}, total steps = ${total}, acceptance rate = ${(accRate*100).toFixed(1)}%\n`+
      `target: 0.5 · N(−2, 0.6²) + 0.5 · N(+2, 0.6²)\n`+
      `tip: σ ≪ 0.5 → high acceptance but stuck in one mode (slow mixing); σ ≫ 3 → bold but rejected; the sweet spot is σ ~ width-of-mode.`;
  }
  sigIn.addEventListener('input',draw);
  $('#mh-step100').addEventListener('click',()=>{ for(let k=0;k<100;k++) step(); draw(); });
  $('#mh-reset').addEventListener('click',()=>{ chain=[0]; accept=0; total=0; draw(); });
  // run a few steps so the page has something to look at on load
  for(let k=0;k<200;k++) step();
  draw();
})();
</script>

7. Connections

Random walks sit at the intersection of probability, spectral theory, and combinatorics — the mixing-time question alone has driven major breakthroughs in each.

Markov chains and stochastic processes

Discrete-time martingales and the optional-stopping theorem give the cleanest proofs of hitting-time identities; probability theory supplies the convergence machinery.

Spectral graph theory

Spectral graph theory is the linear-algebra side: $\lambda_2(L)$ controls mixing, isoperimetric inequalities (Cheeger), expander graphs, and explicit constructions (Ramanujan graphs). The walk on a graph is the discrete Brownian motion.

Algorithms and MCMC

Markov-chain Monte Carlo (Metropolis, Glauber, Gibbs) reduces sampling from intractable distributions to mixing of an explicit chain. Path-coupling and conductance arguments are how rigorous mixing bounds are proved.

Probabilistic and extremal combinatorics

Probabilistic method proofs that go through random-walk concentration; extremal combinatorics applications via spectral / Cheeger arguments.

Open frontiers: rapid mixing of Glauber dynamics for Ising and hard-core models (recent breakthroughs via spectral independence); high-dimensional expansion (HDX) and local-to-global theorems; quantum walks and exotic mixing rates.
